sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, val1 INT, val2 INT, str_val VARCHAR(10));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, val1, val2, str_val) VALUES (1, 10, 20, 'abc'), (2, 5, NULL, 'def'), (3, 15, 30, NULL), (4, NULL, 25, 'xyz');
SELECT exec_close((SELECT AVG(val1) % 7 FROM test_data WHERE str_val IS NOT NULL)), val2 FROM test_data GROUP BY val2 HAVING val2 IS NOT NULL;
SELECT a.id, (SELECT COUNT(*) % 5 FROM test_data b WHERE a.val1 = b.val1) AS alias FROM test_data a;
SELECT str_val, exec_close(SUM(val1 % COALESCE(val2, 1))) FROM test_data GROUP BY str_val HAVING SUM(val1) > 10;
SELECT (SELECT MAX(val1) FROM test_data WHERE val2 IS NOT NULL) % (SELECT MIN(val1) FROM test_data) AS mod_result;
SELECT DISTINCT (val1 * 2 % 3) FROM test_data WHERE val1 IS NOT NULL ORDER BY val1;
CREATE TABLE temp_data (temp_val INT, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
SELECT temp_val, exec_close((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_data) % (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE val2 IS NOT NULL)) FROM temp_data;
